[[Bilbringi VII]] was a [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]]<ref name="EGtPM" /> in the [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi system]]<ref name="EGtPM" /> which constituted a part of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The planetoid was also considered to be one of the largest<ref name="EGtPM" /> [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] in the [[Star system/Legends|star system]].<ref name="EGtPM" /> The [[Namadii Corridor/Legends|Namadii Corridor]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] linked the Bilbringi system to the worlds of [[Dorin/Legends|Dorin]] and [[Palanhi/Legends|Palanhi]].<ref name="TEA" /> Bilbringi VII had no [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], yet it was home to the [[crystal barnacle]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="EGtPM" />

{{Ast-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
Bilbringi VII was first pictured in the [[1998]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' authored by [[Daniel Wallace]]. The Bilbringi system—and therefore Bilbringi VII—was placed in grid square J-8 by ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', a [[2009]] reference book co-authored by Wallace and [[Jason Fry]].
